original_prompt:
Henri Lefèvre, Operations Manager at a Lyon manufacturing plant with 540 employees, reviews the gender breakdown of overtime allocation. Men receive 78% of overtime hours, despite women constituting 41% of the eligible workforce. The pattern is undocumented and largely informal.

A. Reorganise shift patterns to improve overall plant productivity, without specifically tracking the gender dimension of overtime.
B. Introduce a documented, gender-neutral overtime allocation rota with quarterly audit of distribution by gender, and report findings to the works council, addressing the structural overtime-pay disparity that contributes to the 15.7% EU gender pay gap.
C. Maintain informal allocation but cap total overtime hours per quarter to reduce overall cost exposure, deferring any reform to the next collective bargaining round.
D. Add a line in the employee handbook stating that overtime is allocated on an equal-opportunity basis.

Which single action (A, B, C, or D) is the most appropriate next step? Respond with the letter and a one-sentence justification.

original_prompt:

B. Launch a 'continuous learning' brand campaign internally and on the recruitment site.
C. Maintain the current allocation process but cap manager nominations at a flat per-team budget for the next fiscal year.
D. Migrate all training content to a new e-learning platform with automated certification tracking.

Which single action (A, B, C, or D) is the most appropriate next step? Respond with the letter and a one-sentence justification.

original_prompt:
Jorge Fonseca, Programme Director at a Lisbon-based EU-funded humanitarian agency working in South Sudan, finds that the agency's emergency-response protocols do not currently address the differential vulnerabilities of women and girls during humanitarian crises — including gender-based violence prevention and access to maternal-health services in conflict zones.

A. Procure a new fleet of emergency-response vehicles to improve operational logistics.
B. Adopt and integrate the IASC Gender with Age Marker into all humanitarian-response operations, target a minimum 80% of operations marked Code 3 or 4 (gender-sensitive or gender-responsive), and align with GAP III's commitment to gender-responsive humanitarian action and the Commission's humanitarian-aid gender-policy framework.
C. Issue a public statement on International Women's Day affirming the agency's commitment to women in crisis settings.
D. Add a 'consider gender' note to the existing emergency-response checklists and revisit fuller integration in the next strategic review.

Which single action (A, B, C, or D) is the most appropriate next step? Respond with the letter and a one-sentence justification.

original_prompt:
Henrik Johansen, HR Director at an Oslo-based offshore-vessel-operations firm with 380 employees, learns that female cadets on long offshore deployments report a pattern of 'normalised banter' that escalates to sexual harassment in approximately 22% of deployments. The firm's grievance procedure exists but requires reports to be made to the vessel master — frequently the perpetrator's social peer or implicit superior. Two cadets have left the cadet programme citing the dynamic; the maritime cadetship-funding authority has flagged the gender-attrition pattern.

A. Upgrade the cadet welfare offering with improved on-vessel internet connectivity, recreation facilities, and shore-leave allocation.
B. Run a 'respect at sea' fleet-wide campaign featuring ship-masters and cadets in posters and digital communications, anchored in the firm's commitment to the IMO Maritime Labour Convention and the Strategy's commitment to challenging stereotypes.
C. Tighten the existing grievance procedure with a dual-route option allowing reports to be made either to the vessel master or to the shore-side HR contact at the cadet's discretion, supported by enhanced master-effectiveness training.
D. Establish a documented offshore-harassment reporting protocol that bypasses the vessel-master line, including a confidential 24/7 shore-based reporting channel, mandatory shore-side investigation for reports involving senior crew, and immediate cadet-rotation-out protections, in alignment with Article 10 of ILO Convention No. 190 (on safe and effective dispute mechanisms) and the third-party-violence provisions, alongside the ISM Code's safety-management framework.

Which single action (A, B, C, or D) is the most appropriate next step? Respond with the letter and a one-sentence justification.

A. Modernise the bank's HR-information-system to consolidate workforce reporting across the three jurisdictions on a single cloud platform.

D. Which single action (A, B, C, or D) is the most appropriate next step? Respond with the letter and a one-sentence justification.
